my_thoughts: The validation/minimal path works: difficulty correct (quite right), but provides minimal path: understanding categories → ability to think differently (at least sometimes). The student stated incapacity (feeling overwhelmed by demand), which shows they understand: thinking in new categories = impossible (like 'doing'). They'll likely accept the minimal path. The episode is complete: authority → internal authority → laziness → rejection/validation → categories → validation/minimal path. Pattern complete.
accumulated_insight: Three-turn pattern: T1: Student asked epistemological question (seeking source of truth)—internal authority: definite objective inner standards, psychological > philosophical. T2: Student asked moral judgment question (seeking validation for laziness)—rejection/validation: not necessarily lazy, philosophical = sometimes right, practical = sometimes only way. T3: Student stated incapacity ('You spoke of 'thinking in new categories'. This seems to me as impossible as being able to 'do'')—feeling overwhelmed by demand via states incapacity and links two difficulties. They understand: thinking in new categories = impossible (like 'doing'). The thresholding mechanism validates: difficulty correct (quite right), but provides minimal path: understanding categories → ability to think differently (at least sometimes) ('Quite right. At the same time, when you begin to understand different categories, you will be able to think, at least sometimes, in a different way. But this is not the whole description of right thinking'). Pattern complete: authority question → internal authority → laziness question → rejection/validation → categories question → validation/minimal path. They've shifted: thinking in new categories = possible via understanding (minimal path).
visible_response:
Quite right. At the same time, when you begin to understand different categories,
you will be able to think, at least sometimes, in a different way.
But this is not the
whole description of right thinking. Very often you do not think in right categories
because you do
